task_updates.executor_notes: Searched for an existing wallet-block exception and found none in `src/common` or `src/features`, so `WalletUpdateBlockedError` was added to `db_handler.py`. `upsert_wallet` still does the existing-wallet lookup first, and now raises `WalletUpdateBlockedError` only when the existing wallet differs from the incoming address and `has_active_payment_or_intent(guild_id, discord_user_id)` is true; identical-address updates and first-time inserts remain unblocked. Grep of `.upsert_wallet(` in `src/` returned only `admin_chat_cog.py` and `grants_cog.py`, and both callers now catch `WalletUpdateBlockedError` with user-facing fail-closed messages. Verified with `python -m py_compile ...` and `PYTHONPATH=. pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py tests/test_social_publish_service.py tests/test_scheduler.py`.

Before editing, `rg -n "amount_usd" tests/test_admin_payments.py tests/test_social_publish_service.py` found three existing `amount_usd is None` assertions: admin payment flow test payload at `tests/test_admin_payments.py:524` uses provider `solana` in an `is_test` path, the direct amount_token payment test at `tests/test_admin_payments.py:707` uses provider `solana`, and the social publish payment test at `tests/test_social_publish_service.py:496` uses provider `solana_native` with `is_test=True`; all are outside the default capped provider set. `PaymentService.__init__` now accepts `per_payment_usd_cap`, `daily_usd_cap`, `capped_providers`, and `on_cap_breach`, normalizing capped providers to a lowercased frozenset. In `request_payment`, the cap block is gated on `if not is_test and provider_key in self.capped_providers:`; the amount_token-only capped path derives USD via `get_token_price_usd() * amount_token`, stamps both the record and `request_payload` mirrors before `create_payment_request`, leaves uncapped and test paths untouched, fails closed with `last_error='cap check unavailable: token price missing'` when price lookup is unavailable, and triggers `_emit_cap_breach(created)` after insert on cap breach. `execute_payment` was left untouched. Verified with `python -m py_compile ...` and `PYTHONPATH=. pytest tests/test_admin_payments.py tests/test_social_publish_service.py tests/test_scheduler.py`.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Verified `upsert_wallet` still looks up the existing row first, raises `WalletUpdateBlockedError` only on changed-address plus active-payment state, both admin_chat and grants catch it with user-facing messages, and `rg -n "\.upsert_wallet\(" src` showed only those two production callers.

Verified the capped amount_token stamping lives inside `if not is_test and provider_key in self.capped_providers`, derived USD/token price are written to both the record and `request_payload` before insert, uncapped `solana` / `solana_native` paths still avoid extra price calls, missing price fails closed with the exact manual_hold error string, and the post-insert cap-breach callback path fires.
